content.text:
The compiled version branches to return NULL early (beq at offset 8 → offset 20), while the original branches to the end (bne at offset 8 → 0x44 = end). The compiler inverted the condition and put the return NULL early. The original has `bne .L_801F2C08` which skips the whole body. Let me restructure the code to get the compiler to generate the right branch pattern.

The original asm starts with:
```
cmpwi r0, 1
bne .L_801F2C08   (skip to return NULL at end)
```

But compiler generated:
```
cmpwi r0, 1
beq (continue)     (inverted!)
li r3, 0           (return NULL early)
blr
```

This is a classic compiler optimization difference. The compiler sees `if (grMc_804D69D4 != 1) return NULL;` and generates the early-out pattern. I need to restructure so the compiler keeps the original branch direction. Let me try wrapping the body in an `if (grMc_804D69D4 == 1)` block.
